title: Stabilize on New Hardware — setup.sh, Dep Fixes, BodegaRouter Path Matching, Power Topology
motivation: Cloned to new M5 Pro laptop — 14 tests failing due to missing deps (matplotlib, Playwright browsers)
BodegaRouter model ID matching broken: topology uses short alias 'bodega-raptor-90M' but Bodega registers models by full HF path 'srswti/bodega-raptor-90m' when no alias is set
Power topology MID model was bodega-raptor-1b — bodega-vertex-4b is what's actually running on 64 GB machines
No setup script: gap between 'works on old laptop with everything running' and 'works on fresh clone'
pyproject.toml missing matplotlib, numpy, redis as declared dependencies

files_created.description: One-command fresh-clone setup for Apple Silicon. Installs Redis + PostgreSQL 16 via Homebrew (native, no Docker), creates 'octane' database, bootstraps Python venv, installs all deps including matplotlib/numpy, runs playwright install chromium, creates .env from defaults, smoke-tests all services at the end.

changes: Added [project.optional-dependencies] dev = [pytest>=8.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.24, ruff] so pip install -e '.[dev]' works
Power topology MID tier: bodega-raptor-1b (SRSWTI/bodega-raptor-0.9b) → bodega-vertex-4b (SRSWTI/bodega-vertex-4b)
Updated comment: 'M2/M3 Max/Ultra 32 GB+' → 'M2/M3/M4/M5 Max/Ultra 32 GB+'
1. Direct match — topology model_id == loaded model ID (e.g. 'bodega-raptor-90M')
2. Path match — topology.model_path.lower() == loaded_id.lower() — handles Bodega registering models by full HF path (e.g. 'srswti/bodega-raptor-90m') when loaded without explicit alias
3. Any LM fallback — true last resort, logs info-level warning
Root cause: old code fell straight to 'any LM' on path mismatch, collapsing all 3 tiers to the same model
test_router_chat_passes_model_id_to_client: pre-populate router._loaded_models to avoid calling real Bodega health endpoint in unit test
test_topology_power_mid_is_1b → test_topology_power_mid_is_vertex_4b: updated assertion to bodega-vertex-4b
test_router_accepts_topology_object: updated MID model_id assertion from bodega-raptor-1b to bodega-vertex-4b

bugs_fixed.title: BodegaRouter tier routing collapsed — all tiers routed to same model
bugs_fixed.root_cause: _resolve_available_model() did a direct string match of topology model_id ('bodega-raptor-90M') against the loaded model IDs returned by Bodega's /health endpoint. When Bodega loads models without an explicit alias (e.g. via bodega_mlx_engine launch --model srswti/bodega-raptor-90m), it registers them by their full HF path in lowercase. Direct match fails → falls through to 'any LM fallback' → all 3 tiers (FAST, MID, REASON) resolve to the same first-found model. On the old laptop models were loaded with short aliases (matching topology); on the new machine they were not.
bugs_fixed.fix: Added path-based match step: topology.model_path.lower() == loaded_id.lower(). FAST matches srswti/bodega-raptor-90m, REASON matches srswti/bodega-raptor-8b-mxfp4, MID matches srswti/bodega-vertex-4b. Tier routing now works regardless of whether Bodega was launched with an alias or not.
bugs_fixed.impact: QueryStrategist (FAST), Synthesizer (MID), Evaluator (REASON) were all calling the same model. Deep research quality degraded silently.

bugs_fixed.title: 14 test failures on fresh install — missing matplotlib, Playwright browsers
bugs_fixed.root_cause: matplotlib not in pyproject.toml dependencies; Playwright installed as Python package but browser binaries not downloaded (requires separate playwright install chromium step)
bugs_fixed.fix: Added matplotlib and numpy to pyproject.toml. setup.sh runs playwright install chromium automatically. Tests requiring matplotlib (price_chart, portfolio_projection, technical_indicators) and Playwright (browser scraping) now pass.

live_test.command: python3 -c "from octane.main import app; app()" -- health

commands_for_fresh_clone: bash setup.sh                            # installs everything, starts services
next_session: Session 26 — main.py surgery (split 4,057-line file into cli/ modules) + first real integration tests
